2604.08133.title: Alloc-MoE: Budget-Aware Expert Activation Allocation for Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Inference
2604.08133.paper_summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) has become a dominant architecture for scaling large language models due to their sparse activation mechanism. However, the substantial number of expert activations creates a critical latency bottleneck during inference, especially in resource-constrained deployment scenarios. Existing approaches that reduce expert activations potentially lead to severe model performance degradation. In this work, we introduce the concept of \emph{activation budget} as a constraint on the number of expert activations and propose Alloc-MoE, a unified framework that optimizes budget allocation coordinately at both the layer and token levels to minimize performance degradation. At the layer level, we introduce Alloc-L, which leverages sensitivity profiling and dynamic programming to determine the optimal allocation of expert activations across layers. At the token level, we propose Alloc-T, which dynamically redistributes activations based on routing scores, optimizing budget allocation without increasing latency. Extensive experiments across multiple MoE models demonstrate that Alloc-MoE maintains model performance under a constrained activation budget. Especially, Alloc-MoE achieves $1.15\times$ prefill and $1.34\times$ decode speedups on DeepSeek-V2-Lite at half of the original budget.
2604.08133.paper_summary_zh: 混合專家（MoE）已成為擴展大型語言模型的主流架構，因為其稀疏激活機制。然而，大量的專家激活在推理過程中造成了關鍵的延遲瓶頸，特別是在資源受限的部署場景中。現有的減少專家激活的方案可能導致模型性能的嚴重下降。在這項工作中，我們引入了\emph{激活預算}的概念，作為對專家激活數量的約束，並提出了Alloc-MoE，一個統一的框架，協調地在層級和標記級別上優化預算分配，以最小化性能下降。在層級上，我們引入了Alloc-L，它利用敏感性分析和動態規劃來確定跨層的專家激活的最佳分配。在標記級別上，我們提出了Alloc-T，它根據路由分數動態重新分配激活，優化預算分配而不增加延遲。在多個MoE模型上的廣泛實驗表明，Alloc-MoE在受限的激活預算下保持了模型性能。特別是，Alloc-MoE在DeepSeek-V2-Lite上實現了$1.15\times$的預填充和$1.34\times$的解碼加速，預算僅為原始的一半。
2604.08133.author: Baihui Liu et.al.
2604.08133.authors: Baihui Liu, Kaiyuan Tian, Wei Wang, Zhaoning Zhang, Linbo Qiao, Dongsheng Li
